abstract: Facial recognition payment technology (FR) has the potential to disrupt the offline retailing industry by automating the payment process. However, some firms that adopted FR payment technology have experienced only moderate success, and many customers have expressed frustration using FR payment technology. By utilizing data sets from three retail chains, we find that customers are less likely to use FR payment technology during self-checkouts when more customers are in line behind them, waiting and watching (the social presence effect), and when more preceding customers use the other payment technology (the herding effect).  These findings imply that (1) the design of FR technology can be improved to alleviate the social presence effect (such as adding a privacy screen filter or beautify the appearance of the consumer’s image), and (2) monetary incentives may be used to attract more users by leveraging the herding effect.

affiliation: Dongbei University of Finance and Economics
authors_full.name: Jia Gao

affiliation: University of Chinese Academy of Sciences
authors_full.name: Xin Tian

ob.ai_reason: 人脸识别AI核心，消费行为强相关
ob.tldr: 研究利用三家零售链的真实交易数据，考察顾客使用人脸识别支付（FR）时的社会临场感与从众行为。结果显示排队人数越多、身后观察的社交压力越大，顾客越倾向放弃FR支付；同时前序顾客使用其他支付方式的从众效应也会降低FR使用率。论文据此提出可优化FR界面（如加入隐私滤镜或美化形象）与利用从众效应设计激励措施的改进建议。

title: Improving Convenience or Saving Face? An Empirical Analysis of the Use of Facial Recognition Payment Technology in Retail
title_zh: 提升便利还是挽回面子？零售场景中面部识别支付技术使用的实证分析
